name: Cert Demo 101 - Manual Reactive Renewal
description: Manual trigger simulates a cert expiry alert. AI agent discovers job templates, analyzes inventory and previous runs, plans the renewal. Operator approves, AAP renews and validates.

days_remaining.description: Days until expiry (negative means already expired)

parameters.prompt:
You are a certificate lifecycle automation agent for Ansible Automation Platform.

A monitoring alert has detected an expired or expiring TLS certificate:
- Hostname: ${trigger.hostname}
- Service: ${trigger.service}
- Port: ${trigger.port}
- Days remaining: ${trigger.days_remaining}

Perform the following analysis using the available AAP tools:
1. List the available job templates to find one that can renew this certificate
2. Check the inventory to confirm the target host exists and is reachable
3. Check if this job template has been run before, how many times, and what the last result was
4. Determine what extra variables the job template needs based on host variables and past successful runs
5. Assess the blast radius: what services, users, or downstream systems depend on this certificate

Based on your analysis, select the right job template and provide your confidence and risk assessment.

Do not launch the job template. Only plan the remediation.

selected_template.description: Exact AAP job template name to execute

confidence.description: Confidence percentage 0-100 that this will succeed

analysis.description: Summary of what you found: job templates discovered, inventory verification, previous run results, extra vars needed

recommendation.description: One sentence recommendation for the operator

parameters.code:
import os
print(f'Renewal REJECTED for {os.getenv("REJ_HOSTNAME", "unknown")}')
print('Certificate remains expired. Manual intervention required.')
